risk_name: OPEX Escalation — Electricity, Maintenance, and Consumables Exceeding Budget

description.summary: Operating costs exceed the project's financial model projections over the operational life, eroding margins and potentially triggering loan covenant breaches. Electricity (70-75% of OPEX) is the dominant cost, but maintenance, stack replacement, water, and consumables collectively contribute 25-30% and can escalate unexpectedly.
description.detailed: OPEX categories at risk: (1) Electricity cost — covered by RK-GRD-004 (price volatility), (2) Stack replacement cost — covered by RK-TEC-001 (PEM degradation) and RK-TEC-002 (Alkaline carbonate), (3) Maintenance cost — limited operational data for large-scale PEM/Alkaline plants means maintenance budgets are based on OEM estimates and chlor-alkali analogs, not hydrogen-specific data, (4) Water cost — water treatment consumables (RO membranes, EDI modules, ion exchange resin) and wastewater disposal costs, (5) Insurance — hydrogen plant insurance market is immature; premiums may adjust upward after industry incidents, (6) Workforce — specialized hydrogen technicians command salary premiums in competitive markets.
description.root_cause: Limited long-term operational data for large-scale electrolysis. OEM maintenance estimates may be optimistic. Immature insurance market. Competitive labor market for hydrogen skills.
trigger_events: Annual maintenance cost exceeds budget by >20% for two consecutive years
Water treatment consumable replacement interval 30% shorter than OEM specification
Key maintenance event (compressor overhaul, transformer service) costs 50% more than budgeted

assessment.probability_rationale: Some OPEX escalation is nearly certain (electricity) but is accounted for in separate risks. Non-electricity OPEX escalation probability is moderate — limited operational data creates uncertainty but most cost categories have industrial analogs.

preventive_actions.description: Include OPEX contingency of 15% in financial model during feasibility, reducing to 10% at FID after OEM maintenance quotes received
Negotiate long-term service agreements (LTSA) with OEM and major equipment suppliers with capped escalation rates

incident_descriptions: Puertollano (GA-PR-006): Operational since 2022 — accumulating the first multi-year PEM OPEX dataset at 20 MW scale. Iberdrola has not publicly reported OPEX overruns, suggesting maintenance costs are within expectations for a well-designed plant.

HySynergy (GA-PR-007): Operational since 2025. World-first grid balancing demonstration (Dec 2025) provides ancillary revenue that offsets OPEX — a potential OPEX mitigation strategy through grid service revenue.

sources.title: IEA Global Hydrogen Review 2025 — Operating cost analysis
sources.author: IEA
